coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of the penetration testing process and the rules of engagement that authorize an assessment
Elementary knowledge of the OWASP Top 10 web vulnerability categories
Ability to run passive and active reconnaissance with tools like Nmap under supervision
Basic knowledge of common web flaws such as reflected XSS and simple SQL injection
Ability to follow a guided exploitation walkthrough against intentionally vulnerable labs
Understanding of how findings are recorded with evidence and reproduction steps
Documentation of vulnerabilities with severity noted from a provided rubric
Elementary knowledge of remediation advice for the flaws that were found
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with a security-focused Linux distribution and its common tooling
Basic knowledge of Metasploit modules for known exploits
Elementary understanding of Bash or Python scripting to automate small tasks
indicators: Requires constant supervision on every engagement
Executes basic scans and reconnaissance against a defined scope
Is building foundational offensive security skills

coreCompetencies: Ability to carry out a full web application pentest against a defined scope with light supervision
Implementation of thorough reconnaissance including subdomain enumeration and service fingerprinting
Practical knowledge of exploiting the OWASP Top 10 including injection, broken access control and authentication flaws
Ability to escalate privileges on Linux and Windows hosts using common misconfigurations
Understanding of post-exploitation basics such as looting credentials and pivoting
Ability to intercept and manipulate traffic with a proxy like Burp Suite
Knowledge of the OWASP Testing Guide methodology for structured coverage
Ability to perform internal and external network penetration testing
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of testing frameworks such as PTES and OSSTMM
Ability to write reusable scripts to speed up repetitive testing
Understanding of wireless network security assessment
indicators: Executes standard pentests with supervision at key milestones
Identifies and confirms critical vulnerabilities with proof of concept
Writes clear technical findings with reproduction steps

coreCompetencies: Design of a testing plan and methodology tailored to each target's technology stack
Implementation of advanced exploitation chaining multiple weaknesses into a full compromise
Ability to run focused red team activities such as initial access and lateral movement
Capability to emulate a defined adversary's behaviour during an assessment
Ability to develop and adapt custom exploits and payloads for a specific target
Deep knowledge of Active Directory attack paths like Kerberoasting and credential relaying
Implementation of social engineering pretexts including phishing campaigns within scope
Capability to write executive summaries that translate technical risk for leadership
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of basic malware analysis to understand captured samples
Ability to build small offensive tools to fill gaps in existing tooling
Understanding of threat intelligence to inform realistic attack scenarios
indicators: Leads complex application and network pentests
Develops custom exploits when public ones are unavailable
Mentors junior testers on methodology and tooling

coreCompetencies: Architecture of a repeatable penetration testing program with consistent methodology and quality gates
Design of a red team engagement strategy distinct from time-boxed vulnerability-focused pentests
Implementation of purple team exercises that pair offensive actions with detection engineering feedback
Mastery of adversary emulation against mature environments including EDR-monitored endpoints
Capability to run full-scope engagements spanning external, internal, wireless and physical vectors
Deep knowledge of vulnerability research and analysing CVEs for exploitability
Ability to weaponise and safely demonstrate exploitation of previously unknown flaws
Leadership of assessments end to end from scoping through remediation validation
complementaryCompetencies: Experience participating in bug bounty programs
Knowledge of command-and-control frameworks such as Cobalt Strike at an operational level
Implementation of automation to scale reconnaissance and scanning
indicators: Defines the testing strategy for engagements
Leads a red team through a coordinated operation
Is a technical reference for offensive security

coreCompetencies: Technical leadership across an offensive security practice covering pentest and red team services
Design of long-running red team operations with realistic threat scenarios and objectives
Management of a testing team including scheduling, quality review and skills development
Definition of testing methodologies and reporting standards used across the team
Capability to plan multi-week campaigns aligned to specific threat actors
Implementation of recurring purple team exercises to measurably improve detection
Mastery of threat emulation grounded in the MITRE ATT&CK framework
Communication of offensive findings and residual risk to senior stakeholders
complementaryCompetencies: Experience simulating advanced persistent threat tradecraft
Knowledge of threat actor profiles and their typical tooling
Management of the offensive tooling budget and licensing
indicators: Manages a red team and its engagement pipeline
Defines the vision for offensive security services
Presents offensive security outcomes to executives

coreCompetencies: Direction of the offensive security strategy across the organization
Management of red team operations at scale across multiple business units
Definition of an adversary emulation program mapped to the organization's threat model
Leadership of continuous security validation rather than point-in-time testing
Management of a bug bounty or vulnerability disclosure program
Implementation of continuous and automated penetration testing capabilities
Innovation in attack techniques and tradecraft to stay ahead of defenses
Management of an offensive security research effort
complementaryCompetencies: Experience attributing intrusions to known threat actor groups
Knowledge of building and running an offensive research and development function
Leadership within the wider security research community
indicators: Directs the offensive security function
Participates in the broader security strategy
Decides where offensive security investment goes

coreCompetencies: Executive leadership of offensive security across a large organization
Design of a global red team operating model spanning regions and regulations
Transformation of security assurance from periodic testing to continuous validation
Management of offensive security across a large, distributed enterprise
Creation of an adversary-focused, assume-breach mindset across engineering and defense
Definition of the multi-year roadmap for offensive security capabilities
Communication of offensive security value and risk to the board
Influence on how the industry approaches security testing
complementaryCompetencies: Management of substantial offensive security budgets
Experience scaling offensive teams in high-growth companies
Leadership within the professional hacking community
indicators: Reports to the CISO
Leads an offensive security organization of many practitioners
Defines enterprise offensive security strategy

coreCompetencies: Strategic vision for how offensive security validates enterprise defenses
Leadership of continuous security validation as a company-wide discipline
Creation of threat-informed defense by feeding offensive findings into detection and controls
Management of relationships across offensive vendors, researchers and internal teams
Innovation in exploitation and evasion techniques that raise the bar for the field
Definition of next-generation red teaming practices for the organization
Championing of an assume-breach philosophy across leadership
Influence on internal security policy through demonstrated offensive results
complementaryCompetencies: Experience shaping offensive security at large technology organizations
Advisory relationships with offensive security startups and tooling vendors
Recognized technical authority in penetration testing
indicators: Sits on the security leadership team
Sets the direction for offensive security across the company
Is a recognized leader in the offensive security field

coreCompetencies: Visionary leadership of offensive security as a core assurance capability
Transformation of how the enterprise validates its security posture
Definition of an adversary-centric security operating model
Establishment of continuous validation as the default across the organization
Innovation in offensive tradecraft that shapes industry practice
Advocacy for offensive testing as a driver of measurable defensive improvement
Architecture of the organization's overall offensive security program
Influence on security testing standards beyond the company

Recognition as a leading figure in offensive security

indicators: Leads offensive security at the executive level
